properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
properties.tribal_review_required: yes for any external publication or distribution
properties.redistribution_allowed: yes with attribution and TK Notice label
properties.data_steward_note: Phase I stewardship is temporary. Transfer to Lakota Nation stewardship is the intended Phase II outcome.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
properties.tribal_review_required: yes for any external publication or distribution
properties.redistribution_allowed: yes with attribution and TK Notice label
properties.data_steward_note: Phase I stewardship is temporary. Transfer to Lakota Nation stewardship is the intended Phase II outcome.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
properties.tribal_review_required: yes for any external publication or distribution
properties.redistribution_allowed: yes with attribution and TK Notice label
properties.data_steward_note: Phase I stewardship is temporary. Transfer to Lakota Nation stewardship is the intended Phase II outcome.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
properties.tribal_review_required: yes for any external publication or distribution
properties.redistribution_allowed: yes with attribution and TK Notice label
properties.data_steward_note: Phase I stewardship is temporary. Transfer to Lakota Nation stewardship is the intended Phase II outcome.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
properties.tribal_review_required: yes for any external publication or distribution
properties.redistribution_allowed: yes with attribution and TK Notice label
properties.data_steward_note: Phase I stewardship is temporary. Transfer to Lakota Nation stewardship is the intended Phase II outcome.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
properties.tribal_review_required: yes for any external publication or distribution
properties.redistribution_allowed: yes with attribution and TK Notice label
properties.data_steward_note: Phase I stewardship is temporary. Transfer to Lakota Nation stewardship is the intended Phase II outcome.

properties.treaty_territory: 1868 Fort Laramie Treaty He Sapa (Black Hills)
properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)

properties.treaty_status: Unceded Lakota territory. The 1877 Congressional act taking He Sapa was ruled unconstitutional by the US Supreme Court in United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians (1980). The Treaty Nations have declined compensation, maintaining that the land was never legally transferred.
properties.legal_citation: United States v. Sioux Nation of Indians, 448 U.S. 371 (1980)
properties.tk_label_note: Indigenous interests exist in all records describing He Sapa. Contact originating Lakota communities before use. See: https://localcontexts.org/label/tk-notice/
properties.care_collective_benefit: This data describes He Sapa for purposes of historical documentation and environmental accountability. Results should benefit Lakota Nations who hold rights to this territory.
